intuition: Every opening bracket must be closed by the same type in the correct order. A stack naturally enforces this — push opening brackets, and for each closing bracket, check if the top of the stack matches.
approach: Iterate through the string. If the character is an opening bracket, push it. If it's a closing bracket, check if the stack is non-empty and the top matches. If not, return false. After the loop, check if the stack is empty.

complexity.time: O(n) — single pass through the string
complexity.space: O(n) — stack can hold up to n/2 opening brackets

hints: What data structure processes things in LIFO (last-in, first-out) order?
When you see a closing bracket, the most recent unmatched opening bracket must be its pair.
Don't forget to check that the stack is empty at the end — '(' alone is invalid.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check if the stack is empty before popping — a closing bracket with no matching open bracket.
Only checking counts of brackets — '([)]' has equal counts but is invalid.
Not checking if the stack is empty at the end — '(' has no closing bracket.
author: chron303
